title: Charmed Life - Chapter 19 (Fragment 3)
text:
“We’re holding them,” Mr. Saunders said. He was trying to sound encouraging, but he was nervous. The muttering crowd was coming nearer.

“No, we aren’t!” snapped the old lady in mittens. “We can’t do anything more without Millie.”

Cat had a feeling that everyone was trying to fetch Millie. He thought he ought to help, since they needed her so much, but he did not know what to do. Besides, the dragon’s flames were so hot that he needed all his energy not to get burned.

Roger and Julia could not fetch Millie. “What’s wrong?” said Julia. “We’ve always been able to before.”

“All these people’s spells are stopping us,” said Roger.

“Try again,” said Chrestomanci. “I can’t. Something’s stopping me too.”

“Are you joining in the magic?” the dragon asked Cat. Cat was finding the heat of it really troublesome by now. His face was red and sore. But, as soon as the dragon spoke, he understood. He was joining in the magic. Only he was joining in on the wrong side, because Gwendolen was using him again. He was so used to her doing it that he barely noticed. But he could feel her doing it now. She was using so much of his power to stop Chrestomanci fetching Millie that Cat was getting burned.

For the first time in his life, Cat was angry about it. “She’s no business to!” he told the dragon. And he took his magic back. It was like a cool draft in his face.

“Cat! Stop that!” Gwendolen screamed from the crowd.

“Oh, shut up!” Cat shouted back. “It’s mine!”

At his feet, the little spring ran bubbling out of the grass again. Cat was looking down at it, wondering why it should, when he noticed a sort of gladness come over the anxious Family around him. Chrestomanci was looking upwards, and a light seemed to have fallen across his face. Cat turned around and found Millie was there at last. He supposed it was some trick of the hillside that made her look tall as the apple tree. But it seemed no trick that she also looked kind as the end of a long day. She had Fiddle in her arms. Fiddle was draggled and miserable, but purring.

“I’m so sorry,” Millie said. “I’d have come sooner if I’d known. This poor beast had fallen off the garden wall and I wasn’t thinking of anything else.”

Chrestomanci smiled, and let his hand go. He did not seem to need it to hold back the crowd anymore. They stood where they were, and their muttering had stopped. “It doesn’t matter,” he said. “But we must get to work now.”

The Family got to work at once. Cat found it hard to describe or remember afterward just how they did. He remembered claps and peals of thunder, darkness, and mist. He thought Chrestomanci grew taller than Millie, tall as the sky—but that could have been because the dragon got extremely scared and Cat was kneeling in the grass to make it feel safer. From there he saw the Family from time to time, striding about like giants. Witches screamed and screamed. Warlocks and wizards roared and howled. Sometimes there was whirling white rain, or whirling white snow, or perhaps just whirling white smoke, whirling and whirling. Cat was sure the whole garden was spinning, faster and faster. Among the whirling and the whiteness came flying necromancers, or Bernard striding, or Mr. Saunders, billowing, with snow in his hair. Julia ran past, making knot after knot in her handkerchief. And Millie must have brought reinforcements with her. Cat glimpsed Euphemia, the butler, a footman, two gardeners and, to his alarm, Will
Suggins once, breasting the whiteness in the howling, spinning, screaming garden.

The spinning got so fast that Cat was no longer giddy. It was spinning rock-steady, and humming. Chrestomanci stepped out of the whiteness and under the apple tree and held out one hand to Cat. He was wet and windswept, and Cat was still not sure how tall he was. “Can I have some of your dragons’ blood?” Chrestomanci said.

vocabulary.definition: feeling worried or frightened
vocabulary.example: To his alarm, Will Suggins was breasting the whiteness.

vocabulary.definition: experiencing worry or unease about something
vocabulary.example: A sort of gladness came over the anxious Family around him.

vocabulary.definition: only just; almost not
vocabulary.example: He was so used to her doing it that he barely noticed.

vocabulary.definition: swelling out or puffing up in waves or folds
vocabulary.example: Mr. Saunders, billowing, with snow in his hair.

vocabulary.definition: making small bubbles (of liquid) and a continuous gentle noise
vocabulary.example: The little spring ran bubbling out of the grass again.

vocabulary.definition: short sharp sounds, often of hands striking together or thunder
vocabulary.example: He remembered claps and peals of thunder.

vocabulary.definition: the state of being dark; absence of light
vocabulary.example: He remembered darkness and mist.

vocabulary.definition: wet and dirty, especially from being dragged through mud or water
vocabulary.example: Fiddle was draggled and miserable, but purring.

vocabulary.definition: giving someone confidence or hope
vocabulary.example: He was trying to sound encouraging, but he was nervous.

vocabulary.definition: go for and then bring back someone or something
vocabulary.example: Everyone was trying to fetch Millie.

vocabulary.definition: the visible, gaseous part of a fire
vocabulary.example: The dragon’s flames were so hot that he needed all his energy not to get burned.

vocabulary.definition: feeling dizzy or light-headed
vocabulary.example: Cat was no longer giddy as the spinning got so fast.

vocabulary.definition: a feeling of pleasure or happiness
vocabulary.example: A sort of gladness came over the anxious Family around him.

vocabulary.definition: to make a long loud cry like a dog or wolf
vocabulary.example: Warlocks and wizards roared and howled.

vocabulary.definition: take part in an activity with others
vocabulary.example: Are you joining in the magic?

vocabulary.definition: very unhappy or uncomfortable
vocabulary.example: Fiddle was draggled and miserable.

vocabulary.definition: a thin fog with very small drops of water in the air
vocabulary.example: He remembered darkness and mist.

vocabulary.definition: a group of people speaking in low, indistinct voices expressing dissatisfaction or tension
vocabulary.example: The muttering crowd was coming nearer.

vocabulary.definition: loud ringing sounds of bells or thunder
vocabulary.example: He remembered claps and peals of thunder.

vocabulary.definition: additional people sent to support a group in difficulty
vocabulary.example: Millie must have brought reinforcements with her.

vocabulary.definition: to make a deep loud noise
vocabulary.example: Warlocks and wizards roared and howled.

vocabulary.definition: completely steady and unchanging
vocabulary.example: It was spinning rock-steady and humming.

vocabulary.definition: to shout loudly, often because of fear or excitement
vocabulary.example: Gwendolen screamed from the crowd.

vocabulary.definition: magic words or gestures believed to have magical power
vocabulary.example: All these people’s spells are stopping us.

vocabulary.definition: walking with long, decisive steps in various directions
vocabulary.example: He saw the Family striding about like giants.

vocabulary.definition: causing difficulty or annoyance
vocabulary.example: Cat was finding the heat of it really troublesome by now.

vocabulary.definition: rotating rapidly
vocabulary.example: Whirling white rain, or whirling white snow, or whirling white smoke.

vocabulary.definition: exposed to and battered by wind
vocabulary.example: Chrestomanci was wet and windswept under the apple tree.
